title: What to Know About Ali Larijani, Iran’s Top Security Official
summary: One of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s closest confidants, Mr. Larijani emerged as the face of the government after Mr. Khamenei was killed.

title: Sucked Into War, Gulf Countries Face the Limits of U.S. Security Guarantees
summary: Barraged by Iranian attacks and questioning the value of security ties with the United States, nations in the Gulf have turned to Ukraine, Australia and Italy for help.

title: Admiral’s Comments Undercut Pentagon’s Cluster Munition Policy
summary: The first Trump administration defended cluster munitions as “legitimate,” but on Monday, Adm. Brad Cooper condemned them as “inherently indiscriminate.”

title: U.S. Allies Reject Trump’s Demands for Warships, and Bovino to Retire From Border Patrol
summary: Plus, the champion of “Swedish death cleaning” dies at 91.

title: Hundreds Dead in Pakistani Airstrike on Kabul, Officials in Afghanistan Say
summary: The attack hit a drug rehabilitation facility, Afghanistan said, suggesting that its victims included civilians. Pakistan said it had targeted an ammunitions depot.

title: Why Are Pakistan and Afghanistan Fighting?
summary: The renewed violence between the neighboring countries stems from Pakistan’s accusations that Afghanistan’s Taliban government has harbored a militant group.

title: War Isn’t the Only Thing on Trump’s Mind
summary: President Trump spoke to journalists for about 40 minutes before holding a news conference on Monday, offering a glimpse into his priorities.

title: Richard Grenell: From Trump Cabinet Hopeful to Ex-Kennedy Center Manager
summary: Richard Grenell once hoped to be President Trump’s secretary of state. Instead, Mr. Trump just replaced him as Kennedy Center president.

title: Oil Begins Flowing Through California Pipeline Under Trump Order
summary: Oil had not flowed through the pipeline since a 2015 rupture caused an environmental disaster on California’s Central Coast. It sets up a new fight between the Trump administration and state officials.

title: Trump Officials Weigh New Plan to Stop Offshore Wind Farms
summary: Proposed settlements would block wind farms off New York State and North Carolina, according to documents reviewed by The New York Times.

title: Cuba Ready to Accept Outside Investment, Top Official Says
summary: President Trump’s words came amid a nationwide blackout and as a top Cuban official said his country would move to open the economy to foreign investors.

title: Trump Administration Seeks to Remove Cuba’s President From Power During Negotiations
summary: The United States has told Cuba that for meaningful progress to be made in negotiations, President Miguel Díaz-Canel must step down, said people familiar with the talks.

title: The Mobile, Ala., Leprechaun Lives On 20 Years Later
summary: The purported sighting in March 2006, covered by a local TV news station, made a lasting impression on the internet — and on Mobile, Ala.

title: Inside the Supply Line Delivering American Guns to Mexican Cartels
summary: A surge of weapons is flowing from the U.S. to Mexico. These firearms — sourced from gun shops, shows, websites and apps — are funneled across the border to fuel the country’s most violent crimes.

title: War in Iran Shows Growing Conservative Divide Over Israel
summary: As the U.S.-Israel-Iran war continues, conservatism’s most famous figures  are in a rhetorical brawl over America’s role.

title: Senate to Take Up Voter Bill Sought by Trump but Opposed by Democrats
summary: The Republican leader said he couldn’t overcome a filibuster but, under relentless pressure from the president and the far right, would put Democrats on the record against the restrictive voter I.D. bill.

title: What to Watch in Tuesday’s Illinois Primaries — and Where the Money Went
summary: An open Senate seat and several crowded House races could measure the power of the grass roots to take on the deep pockets of super PACs.

title: Trapped in a Self-Driving Car During an Anti-Robot Attack
summary: In San Francisco, some passengers of autonomous taxis have experienced an unexpected hazard: being stuck in the vehicles when the cars are assaulted.

title: Driverless Big Rigs Are Coming to American Highways, and Soon
summary: There are active test runs in Texas, and a handful of companies are banking on making a big entry into the market as early as next year.

title: Wired’s New Editor Doesn’t Care if the Tech Bros Are Mad
summary: Katie Drummond has transformed the publication into a bright spot for Condé Nast with aggressive political reporting.

title: As Carney Seeks New Alliances for Canada, He Looks Away on Human Rights
summary: Canada’s prime minister chooses pragmatism in a turbulent world, which means doing business with countries that do not share Canada’s democratic values. Some critics see this as weakness.

title: As Income Gap Grows, So Do Fears Over Access to a Quality Education
summary: Leaders and parents worry that a widening economic divide amid the current affordability crisis could amplify the role that money plays in access to a robust education in New York.

title: How Readers Voted on Miscast Roles in the Movies
summary: Given a list of 14 mismatches, they quickly picked Ben Platt in “Dear Evan Hansen.” But they took issue (loudly) with one of our choices.

title: How Michael B. Jordan Won (and Timothée Chalamet Lost) Best Actor at 2026 Oscars
summary: The “Sinners” star became the consensus pick as voters soured on Timothée Chalamet, the “Marty Supreme” lead. It helped that the winner is a bankable performer.

title: Cannonball with Wesley Morris: What the Oscars Got Right
summary: Giving Michael B. Jordan and “KPop Demon Hunters” their due, to start.

title: At Least 23 Killed in Nigeria as Insurgent Attacks Go On Despite U.S. Help
summary: A teaching hospital and two markets in the city of Maiduguri were hit in what a military spokesman said were suicide bombings by Boko Haram.

title: Inside the Katseye Fandom: Friendships, Rivalries and Boycotts Over Manon’s Hiatus
summary: Fans of the group have leveraged their influence into friendship and rivalry. Then a member’s sudden hiatus sent them into a spiral.

title: Trump’s Dangerous Lack of a Strategy in Iran
summary: The early reality of the Iran war is not cooperating with President Trump’s bluster.

title: The Scrappy Mayor Showing Democrats How It’s Done
summary: Democrats can’t just sit back and expect the prevailing political winds to produce a blue wave.

title: Jimmy Kimmel Has a Bone to Pick With Trump’s War Plans
summary: “The only war Trump had an exit plan for was Vietnam,” Kimmel remarked after the president said he would end the war in Iran when he “feels it in his bones.”

title: How To Bring a Bird’s Song Back from the Edge of Extinction
summary: In a new study, wild regent honeyeaters became vocal tutors, teaching their disappearing song to birds in a captive breeding program.

title: In Rural New York, Some See Proposed A.I. Center as a Needless Intrusion
summary: The data center, to be built between Buffalo and Rochester, will raise electric bills and harm a nearby tribal reservation, opponents argue. “I can’t think of one good reason for it,” a local resident said.

title: A Red Fox’s Fantastic Voyage Starts in England and Leads to the Bronx
summary: The fox is convalescing at the Bronx Zoo after an unlikely 3,600-mile sea crossing from England to the Port of New York and New Jersey.

title: March Has Always Been a Month of Weather Extremes. But This Is Ridiculous.
summary: Snow, tornadoes, record-breaking heat, a dust storm — and that’s just last weekend. Here’s how to understand what’s going on, and the role climate change is playing in all of it.
